author: jtraglia
content: Sorry I didn't realize you were OOO. Maybe <@586161934425128960> can respond instead. Why would you set `CAPELLA_FORK_EPOCH` to zero here though? In the test, we set it to 1 so we can specify a pre-capella time. See:

author: nflaig
content: I didn't understand why that was an issue tbh

author: jtraglia
content: I think it's an issue because we do not override `*_FORK_EPOCH` values for other forks.

author: jtraglia
content: So it would pass if you ran it in capella, but fail if you ran it in deneb.

author: jtraglia
content: Because you're using those values to determine what the current fork is.

author: jtraglia
content: I suggested using the manifest (or test filepath) to determine the fork.

author: jtraglia
content: But it doesn't sound like that's a trivial thing to do. Not sure.

author: jtraglia
content: I'll try to find some time today to see if we can get it working without removing this check.

author: nflaig
content: we should probably just load the manifest file and set up our config, would need to see exactly what the issue was, or we wait for NC

author: jtraglia
content: Do you know when NC will be back?

author: jtraglia
content: That's fine. Let's wait until then.

author: nflaig
content: <@592004585506340885> https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5146 is the zip there the latest spec tests we should be passing?

author: jtraglia
content: Yes & no. Let me merge the https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5201 without the capella changes first, then I'll regenerate them. Will ping you when that's ready.

author: nflaig
content: ah right, good that I asked, we definitely need that one

author: jtraglia
content: Okay the tests [here](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5146#issuecomment-4304639223) should be up-to-date now.
